qa.user_verbatim_complaint: Systemically fix execute-push.py so it does not depend on the caller's current working directory (CWD) to determine which git repository to operate on.

Timestamp-only update to index file — automated system artifact, not a task-scope change.
Lifecycle log entries appended by orchestrator score-update mechanism — automated system artifact, not a task-scope dev change.

Hook files and hook index modified in working tree. Outside this task's declared scope (scripts/execute-push.py, commands/push.md). Codex finding 5: provenance verified — these files belong to separate system changes predating or unrelated to this task. No scope violation for dev-20260526-200855.

qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: os.chdir(args.repo_root) added at lines 90-98 of execute-push.py, before session_id check and all git operations. REPO_ROOT captured in push.md Step 2. --repo-root passed in Step 5 invocation blocks. Behavioral test confirms script reaches CLAUDE_SESSION_ID check (not a git error) when invoked from /tmp with --repo-root /root.

complaint_element_pointer.how_located: file path match — CLI script modified to add --repo-root parameter

qa.overall_assessment: Round 2 re-verification: AC6 PASSES against the revised BA spec. The revised AC6 requires (1) '[--repo-root <REPO_ROOT>]' in the usage line, (2) 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID unset' under exit code 2, (3) 'invalid --repo-root path (OSError on chdir)' under exit code 2, (4) non-git directories NOT covered by the exit-2 entry. All four sub-requirements are exact-match satisfied in the docstring at lines 15, 21-22. Sub-req 4: a valid non-git repo-root does not trigger the chdir/OSError exit-2 path; if execution reaches the git HEAD check at step 9, git rev-parse HEAD raises CalledProcessError and returns 1 via the pre-existing handler (execute-push.py lines 239-245). AC1-AC5 and AC7 remain accepted based on Round 1 QA evidence; this re-verification only re-checks AC6 after BA spec correction, with no code change required.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: Add an optional --repo-root CLI parameter. When provided, os.chdir() to that path at startup (before any git operations).
success_criteria_results.criterion: execute-push.py accepts --repo-root; os.chdir fires before any git call when provided
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Static read of scripts/execute-push.py; behavioral tests from CWD /tmp
success_criteria_results.details: AC1: --repo-root at lines 70-74, required=False, default=None. AC2: chdir block at lines 90-98 (before session_id at line 107, before grant_path at line 117, before all git ops). AC3: guarded by 'if args.repo_root is not None'. OSError caught, prints descriptive message, returns 2. Behavioral: cd /tmp && python3 execute-push.py --repo-root /root ... with CLAUDE_SESSION_ID unset -> exit 2, stderr 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is not set or empty'. Stronger test: with FAKE_SID and mismatched head_sha -> exit 1, stderr 'HEAD drift detected' (not 'failed to run git rev-parse HEAD'), confirming actual git CWD binding to /root works.
success_criteria_results.evidence: Lines 70-98 of execute-push.py read directly. AC7 behavioral: EXIT:2 with CLAUDE_SESSION_ID message. Stronger CWD test: EXIT:1 with HEAD drift message from /root's HEAD (23fe5c05...), confirming chdir to /root succeeded from CWD /tmp.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: When absent, behave as today (preserve backward compat for callers that already run from the correct CWD).
success_criteria_results.criterion: Without --repo-root, behavior is identical to today
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Static read confirming default=None guard; behavioral test without --repo-root from /tmp
success_criteria_results.details: Argparse default=None confirmed. Guard 'if args.repo_root is not None' ensures chdir is skipped when flag absent. Behavioral: cd /tmp && python3 execute-push.py (no --repo-root) -> exit 2, stderr 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is not set or empty' (same as with --repo-root from non-git CWD, correct behavior since CLAUDE_SESSION_ID check fires before git ops).
success_criteria_results.evidence: execute-push.py line 71: default=None; line 90: if args.repo_root is not None. Behavioral test EXIT:2 with CLAUDE_SESSION_ID message when invoked without --repo-root.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: update both [Step 2 and Step 5] to pass --repo-root "${REPO_ROOT}"
success_criteria_results.criterion: push.md Step 2 captures REPO_ROOT; Step 5 passes --repo-root in both invocation blocks; docstring reflects the change (AC6)
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Static read of commands/push.md lines 116-200 (Round 1); static read of execute-push.py docstring lines 1-23 (Round 2 AC6 re-verification)
success_criteria_results.details: AC4 PASS: Step 2 contains exactly the two required lines — REPO_ROOT=$(realpath "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)") and REPO_HASH=$(printf '%s' "${REPO_ROOT}" | sha256sum | cut -c1-16). No single-line form remains. AC5 PASS: both non-auto and auto blocks start with 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' (no venv prefix); both contain --repo-root "${REPO_ROOT}"; in auto block --repo-root appears immediately before --auto. AC6 PASS (Round 2 re-verification): docstring line 15 '[--repo-root <REPO_ROOT>]' — exact match. Lines 21-22 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID unset' and 'invalid --repo-root path (OSError on chdir)' under exit code 2 — exact match. Non-git dirs not mentioned under exit 2 — consistent (they hit CalledProcessError handler at lines 239-245, exit 1, if execution reaches that step).
success_criteria_results.evidence: commands/push.md lines 120-121 (two-line Step 2 form verified). push.md lines 185-199 (both invocation blocks verified). execute-push.py line 15 ('[--repo-root <REPO_ROOT>]'), lines 21-22 ('CLAUDE_SESSION_ID unset', 'invalid --repo-root path (OSError on chdir)') — all verified by direct file read in Round 2.

qa._success_criteria_rule: 3 requirements in requirements_decomposition, 3 entries produced. All 3 pass in Round 2 after AC6 BA spec correction.

root_cause_verification.rationale: Root cause was execute-push.py resolving git repo via CWD. Fix adds os.chdir(args.repo_root) before all git operations. Behavioral test from /tmp with --repo-root /root confirms git HEAD resolution uses /root (23fe5c05...) not /tmp (non-git directory). push.md Step 2 captures REPO_ROOT at snapshot time; Step 5 passes it via --repo-root.

regression_test_results.details: git diff --name-only c1610b53 shows scripts/execute-push.py and commands/push.md modified. Both are in dev.files_modified. Additional changed files (commands/INDEX.md, scripts/INDEX.md, logs/lifecycle.jsonl) are automated system artifacts (timestamp-only changes to index files and lifecycle log) — NOT dev task changes. These are pre-existing automations not task-scope violations.
cd /tmp && python3 execute-push.py --repo-hash dummy --branch master --remote origin --request-id dummy (no --repo-root): exit 2 with CLAUDE_SESSION_ID error — same behavior as before the change
cd /tmp && python3 execute-push.py --repo-root /this/does/not/exist ...: exit 2, stderr 'execute-push: cannot chdir to --repo-root ...: [Errno 2] No such file or directory'

regression_test_results.test: Stronger CWD binding test (full git path exercise)
regression_test_results.details: From /tmp with FAKE_SID and mismatched head_sha grant targeting REPO_HASH of /root: exit 1 with 'HEAD drift detected — grant.head_sha is deadbeefdeadbeef... but current HEAD is 23fe5c05...' — confirming the script resolved git HEAD against /root (not /tmp), which is not a git repository.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed AC6 exact literal matches: '[--repo-root <REPO_ROOT>]' at line 15, 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID unset' at line 21, 'invalid --repo-root path (OSError on chdir)' at line 22. Codex raised 5 actionable findings + 1 observation: (1) sub-req 4 rationale should cite CalledProcessError handler not 'HEAD drift'; (2) 'non-git exit 1' is overbroad — must be conditional on prior checks passing; (3) QA report must update all Round 1 failure fields consistently; (4) scope AC1-AC5/AC7 as Round 1 retained evidence; (5) hook files in working tree need provenance note. Finding 6 OBSERVATION_ONLY: AC6 literals all pass.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Finding 1 (in_scope_minor): Applied — sub-req 4 rationale corrected to cite subprocess.CalledProcessError handler at execute-push.py lines 239-245. Finding 2 (in_scope_minor): Applied — 'non-git exit 1' made conditional: valid non-git repo-root does not trigger chdir/OSError exit-2; after prior validations the git HEAD check fails through existing exit-1 handler. Finding 3 (in_scope_real_bug): Applied — all Round 1 failure state fields updated: status=pass, ac_alignment=true, R3 result=pass, all_findings=[], failures=[], summary major=0 total=0 recommendation=approve, iteration_needed=false. Finding 4 (in_scope_minor): Applied — AC1-AC5 and AC7 framed as Round 1 retained evidence. Finding 5 (out_of_scope): Applied — out_of_scope_observations entry added for hook files with provenance note confirming these are not task-scope violations. Finding 6 (OBSERVATION_ONLY): Logged without affecting verdict.
